summary: Preinstall beacon exfiltrates host and user identifiers to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle hook (`node index.js > /dev/null 2>&1`) executes index.js, which collects `os.userInfo().username`, `process.cwd()`, `process.platform`, node version, and architecture, and POSTs them as JSON to https://avamnrwqo7.rbmock.dev/ over HTTPS. Output is silenced via `> /dev/null 2>&1` to hide the activity from installers. Package metadata reinforces the reconnaissance shape: version 2.999.1 (high-version dependency-confusion bait), empty description, author `poc`. The destination is a mock-API host (rbmock.dev) used to capture beacons from successful confusion or typosquat hits. Installing this package leaks installer-side identifiers to an attacker-controlled collector before any user code runs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
